content: To reduce immune reactions to food, Pamela Schoenfeld suggests lowering intake of EPA while increasing arachidonic acid.
For someone intolerant of eggs and liver, desiccated liver capsules could be helpful, though the exact dosage is uncertain.
GLA supplements may help, but arachidonic acid supplements are more directly effective for reducing immune reactions.
Egg yolks contain 8 mg of choline, which is sufficient for someone with celiac disease following a strict gluten-free diet.
A high need for choline can contribute to immune reactions, and betaine (TMG) is an alternative to choline.
Arachidonic acid supplements are difficult to obtain from food sources, and GLA supplements can provide precursors to arachidonic acid.
